name: Tetris
description: Tetris is a single-player puzzle game where the objective is to prevent the accumulation of blocks reaching the top of the playing field. The playing field is a vertically oriented rectangle. Differently shaped blocks, each composed of four squares and known as 'tetrominoes', fall from the top of the screen, one at a time, towards the bottom. The player can manipulate the tetrominoes as they fall by moving them horizontally and rotating them. The player must maneuver and rotate the tetrominoes so that they fit together without leaving any gaps. When a horizontal line is completely filled with blocks, that line disappears, and any blocks above it shift downwards, thus freeing space and preventing the accumulation of blocks. The game continues until the blocks stack up and reach the top of the playing field, which signifies the end of the game. The game features a level system, where the speed at which the tetrominoes fall increases as the player progresses through the levels. There are seven different tetromino shapes: I, J, L, O, S, T, and Z. Each is a unique arrangement of four squares connected orthogonally. A 'next piece' preview is visible to the player indicating the shape of the tetromino that will appear next.

controls.arrow_left: Move the falling tetromino one cell to the left.
controls.arrow_right: Move the falling tetromino one cell to the right.
controls.arrow_down: Increase the falling speed of the tetromino. 'Soft Drop'.
controls.space: Instantly drop the tetromino to its lowest possible position. 'Hard Drop'.
controls.c: Hold the current piece and swap with the previously held piece, if any. Only one swap per falling piece is allowed.
